title: RSVP Responses Button, Nav Text Contrast Fix & Mobile Music Controller Fix

requirements.text: Add a prominent dedicated 'View RSVP Responses' button on each invitation card in the creator dashboard (/dashboard) and inside the invitation editor (/dashboard/:slug). Clicking it opens a modal or expands a panel showing all submitted RSVP guest responses for that invitation fetched via getRSVPsByInvitation. Display: Guest Name, Phone, Attending (Yes/No badge), Guest Count, Message, and Submitted At date in a clean styled table/card list. Include a summary row with total responses, total confirmed guests, and total declined. Show a graceful empty state when no responses exist. Style the button prominently (gold/crimson accent, distinct from other action buttons) so it stands out as a special action.

quotes: yes create a special button for that were it shows that

acceptanceCriteria: A visually distinct 'View RSVP Responses' button appears on each invitation card on the /dashboard page
The same button appears inside the invitation editor at /dashboard/:slug
Clicking the button opens a modal or expandable panel showing all RSVP submissions
The panel displays Guest Name, Phone, Attending badge (Yes=green/No=red), Guest Count, Message, and Submitted At
A summary row shows total responses, total confirmed guests, and total declined
An elegant empty state is shown when no RSVPs have been submitted
The button uses a premium gold/crimson accent color distinct from Edit, Preview, Publish, and Delete buttons
Data is fetched using the existing getRSVPsByInvitation React Query hook pattern

requirements.text: Fix the navigation link text visibility in the site header (Header.tsx) on the home/landing page. The 'Home' and 'Dashboard' nav link text is currently hidden/invisible against the dark or image-based background. Apply a light text color (ivory/white or gold) to all navigation links when the header is in its transparent (top-of-page) state, and ensure the text remains readable in the scrolled/filled state as well. Add a subtle semi-transparent dark backdrop or a distinct border/boundary around the navigation links if needed to guarantee contrast on all backgrounds. Update hover states to match the new color scheme.

quotes: on home tab 'home' and 'dashboard wriiten is hides due to background fix it or makes it s own boundry or fix it

acceptanceCriteria: 'Home' and 'Dashboard' nav link text is clearly legible against the landing page hero background in both transparent and scrolled header states
Navigation links use a light color (ivory/white or gold) in the transparent header state
Text remains readable after the header gains a background on scroll
If a backdrop or boundary treatment is used, it is consistent with the luxury aesthetic
Hover states are visible and use appropriate gold/blush accent colors
Fix applies on both desktop and mobile navigation

requirements.text: Fix the floating background music controller (MusicController.tsx) so it activates and functions correctly on mobile devices. Currently the music controller does not open or respond to taps on mobile (works on desktop only). Ensure: the floating button has a sufficiently large touch target (at least 44x44px), tap events are handled correctly (use onClick which works for both touch and mouse), autoplay restrictions on mobile are handled by requiring a user gesture before starting audio, the play/pause state toggles correctly on tap, and the controller panel opens/closes on mobile. Test that audio actually plays after the user taps play on a mobile browser.

quotes: in mobile music not opens directly in laptop it working

acceptanceCriteria: The floating music controller button is tappable on mobile browsers (iOS Safari, Android Chrome)
Tapping the floating button opens/closes the music controller panel on mobile
The touch target for the button is at least 44x44px
Audio plays after the user taps the play button on mobile (respects mobile autoplay restrictions by requiring user gesture)
Play/pause state toggles correctly on repeated taps
The controller remains functional and visible on small screens without layout overflow
Music controller continues to work on desktop/laptop as before

constraints: Do not modify files listed in frontend.immutablePaths
All backend calls must use existing React Query hooks in useQueries.ts
Do not change the backend data model or Motoko actor
nonGoals: Adding new backend functions — use existing getRSVPsByInvitation
Redesigning the entire header or navigation structure
Adding authentication or login to the dashboard
Changing the music file storage mechanism
